Container.Name: khord
Container.Overview:
Khord is a decentralized music sharing platform built on the AT Protocol (Bluesky). Share songs once — your followers listen on whichever streaming service they use (Apple Music, Spotify, YouTube Music, Deezer, and more). Setlists, voting, cross-platform links, and an owner admin panel all included. No central database — records live in each user's AT Protocol PDS.
No Caddy/reverse proxy is included — point your existing Unraid reverse proxy (NGINX Proxy Manager, Swag, Traefik, etc.) at port 3000 on this container. AT Protocol OAuth requires a publicly accessible HTTPS URL, so a reverse proxy with a valid TLS certificate is required.
Also install the khord-indexer container (same Data Path) to enable the AppView feed. Without it the app still works via direct PDS fetching.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port exposed by the container. Point your reverse proxy (NGINX Proxy Manager, Swag, etc.) at this port — do not expose it directly to the internet.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: Directory for the SQLite database. Must match the Data Path set in the khord-indexer container.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Publicly accessible URL for this instance, e.g. https://khord.yourdomain.com. Must match the AT Protocol OAuth redirect URI.

Config.Name: Spotify Client ID
Config.Description: Spotify app client ID from developer.spotify.com. Optional — enables Spotify URL resolution when sharing songs. Both Client ID and Client Secret are required together.

Config.Name: Spotify Client Secret
Config.Description: Spotify app client secret from developer.spotify.com. Required alongside Spotify Client ID. Keep this private.

Config.Name: YouTube API Key
Config.Description: YouTube Data API v3 key from console.cloud.google.com. Optional — enables YouTube Music URL resolution when sharing songs. Also enable YouTube Music in the admin panel Settings tab. Note: free quota is ~100 searches/day (100 units per call, 10,000/day limit).

Config.Name: Owner DIDs
Config.Description: Comma-separated AT Protocol DIDs granted admin/owner privileges. Owners get access to the /admin panel. Find your DID at: https://bsky.social/xrpc/com.atproto.identity.resolveHandle?handle=your.handle

Config.Name: Allowed DIDs
Config.Description: Comma-separated AT Protocol DIDs allowed to sign in. Leave empty for open registration.

Config.Name: Banned DIDs
Config.Description: Comma-separated AT Protocol DIDs blocked from signing in. Requires restart. For live bans without restart, use the /admin panel.

Config.Name: Max Users
Config.Description: Maximum number of registered users. 0 = unlimited. Can also be adjusted live from the /admin panel.

Config.Name: App Name
Config.Description: Display name shown in the UI and page titles.

Config.Name: App Tagline
Config.Description: One-line tagline shown on the home page.

Config.Name: Auth Provider Name
Config.Description: Identity provider name shown in sign-in UI copy.

Config.Name: Disable Album Art
Config.Description: Set to true to hide album art thumbnails on song cards. Can also be toggled live from the /admin panel.

Config.Name: Theme
Config.Description: UI color theme. Requires a container restart to apply. Options — neutral dark: dark, zinc, slate, gray, neutral, stone. Neutral light: light, zinc-light, slate-light, neutral-light, stone-light. Chromatic dark: navy, teal, emerald, rose, violet.

Config.Name: Database Path
Config.Description: Full path to the SQLite database file inside the container. The directory must match the Data Path mount. The filename rarely needs changing.

Config.Name: Thumbnail Cache Directory
Config.Description: Directory for the server-side album art disk cache. Lives inside the Data Path volume by default. Manage cache size from the admin panel (Admin → Cache tab).
